mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 8.0, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand recovery, which accelerates corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a win rate above 50 percent, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production indicate immediate supply-side bottlenecks and reduced economic activity, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth fails to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction during liquidity-driven market rallies.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening labor demand and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.015.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPI exceeds 2.5 percent annually, anticipated central bank tightening raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Elevated CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or equity markets price in inflation tolerance resulting in positive forward returns despite high CPI.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP growth and stagnant retail sales indicates weak household demand transmission, which suppresses corporate earnings revisions and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence interaction term shows no statistical significance in predicting 20-day returns, or the signal fails to outperform a baseline model in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid expansion in industrial production combined with low unemployment signals an overheating economy, prompting short-term profit-taking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week during tight labor regimes.
failure_condition: High INDPRO growth during low unemployment periods fails to predict negative 5-day returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.
